I INTRODUCTION 
I.1 Product name  
OMI_EXTREME_SST_BALTIC_sst_mean_and_anomaly_obs 
OMI_EXTREME_SST_NORTHWESTSHELF_sst_mean_and_anomaly_obs 
OMI_EXTREME_SST_WAVE_IBI_sst_mean_and_anomaly_obs 
OMI_EXTREME_SST_MEDSEA_sst_mean_and_anomaly_obs 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
The EXTREME SST indicators are based on the computation of the 99th and the 
1st percentiles (that represent approximately the 1% highest and lowest values, 
respectively) from in situ data (observations), following the same approach 
applied by Woodworth and Blackman (2004) to extreme sea levels. This 
method determines changes in the frequency distribution of the measured 
variables. 
All the data used for the computation of these indicators have been 
downloaded from Copernicus Marine Service In Situ Thematic Assembly Centre 
(In Situ TAC ) products. The analysis is performed for the following variable: sea 
surface temperature (sst) measured by in situ buoys.  
The periods selected are based on our analysis of the data available in the 
different In Situ TAC  regions that fulfilled specific criteria of data completeness 
and quality (Pérez Gómez et al 2016, Pérez Gómez et al 2018). Buoys were 
selected according to consolidated time series of hourly data in Copernicus 
Marine Service for the period 2000 to the target year. Within these periods, a 
minimum number of 10 years with completeness index larger than 70% is 
required for a station to be included.  These numbers can be reduced to 8 
years and 60% in case that the coverage is not enough to make the OMIs 
properly representative. These data were then used to compute annually the 
99th and 1st percentile levels at each station. 
More details can be found in the Copernicus Marine Service  Ocean State 
Report (Pérez Gómez et al., 2016; Pérez Gómez et al., 2018).

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document outlines the evaluation method for Ocean Monitoring Indicators (OMI) related to extreme sea surface temperatures (SST) in various ocean regions, using percentile analysis of in situ data from Copernicus Marine Service In Situ Thematic Assembly Centre products.
